content.problemStatement:
The **Fibonacci sequence** F0,F1,…F_0, F_1, \ldotsF0​,F1​,… is a special infinite sequence of non-negative integers, where F0=0F_0 = 0F0​=0, F1=1F_1 = 1F1​=1 and for each integer n≥2n \ge 2n≥2, Fn=Fn−1+Fn−2F_n = F_{n-1} + F_{n-2}Fn​=Fn−1​+Fn−2​.

Consider the sequence DDD of the last decimal digits of the first NNN Fibonacci numbers, i.e. D=(F0%10,F1%10,…,FN−1%10)D = (F_0 \% 10, F_1 \% 10, \ldots, F_{N-1} \% 10)D=(F0​%10,F1​%10,…,FN−1​%10). Now, you should perform the following process:


Let D=(D1,D2,…,Dl)D = (D_1, D_2, \ldots, D_l)D=(D1​,D2​,…,Dl​).
If l=1l = 1l=1, the process ends.
Create a new sequence E=(D2,D4,…,D2⌊l/2⌋)E = (D_2, D_4, \ldots, D_{2 \lfloor l/2 \rfloor})E=(D2​,D4​,…,D2⌊l/2⌋​). In other words, EEE is the sequence created by removing all odd-indexed elements from DDD.
Change DDD to EEE.
When this process terminates, the sequence DDD contains only one number. You have to find this number.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the last remaining number.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The first NNN Fibonacci numbers are (0,1,1,2,3,5,8,13,21)(0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21)(0,1,1,2,3,5,8,13,21). The corresponding sequence DDD is (0,1,1,2,3,5,8,3,1)(0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 3, 1)(0,1,1,2,3,5,8,3,1).

Remove all elements at odd indices of DDD: (0,1,1,2,3,5,8,3,1)→(1,2,5,3)(0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 3, 1) \rightarrow (1, 2, 5, 3)(0,1,1,2,3,5,8,3,1)→(1,2,5,3).
Remove all elements at odd indices of DDD: (1,2,5,3)→(2,3)(1, 2, 5, 3) \rightarrow (2, 3)(1,2,5,3)→(2,3).
Remove all elements at odd indices of DDD: (2,3)→(3)(2, 3) \rightarrow (3)(2,3)→(3).

The remaining element is 333.
